purpose: Freeze the Node Script and Executor runtime, stop, log, authorization and live snapshot semantics before the Go process runtime is enabled. This contract describes a migration boundary; it does not authorize a second runtime to execute or terminate an existing process.

safety.fixture_inputs: repository-owned sanitized metadata and placeholders only
forbidden_fixture_content: command, inline body or argument value
generator_rules: Never start Electron, sql.js, a process, a scheduler or a plugin.
Never read userData, a project workspace, process environment values or a database.
Only write an explicitly selected artifact below fixtures/migration/p12.
Normalize ids, timestamps, paths, pids, logs and process-specific values to placeholders.

unchanged: Script CRUD, validation, ordering and tasks.json Executor import remain Node/static behavior until their separately scoped migration work.
DesktopBridge file selection remains a privileged DesktopBridge concern and is not part of Script or Executor runtime execution.
Existing AppSnapshot collection presence and non-runtime business semantics remain unchanged; P12 freezes only runtime, stop, log, Operation and SSE behavior.
compatibility_boundary.blocked_when_unfrozen: Any Script or Executor execution branch, stop path, output path, event path or ownership rule not represented below blocks P12 migration rather than receiving an implicit Go fallback.

runtime_ownership.rule: The runtime that creates a Script or Executor operation owns its registered process, cancellation and final state until that operation is terminal. A feature-flag change, application restart or a later request cannot move that ownership to the other runtime.
runtime_ownership.node_processes_after_go_enable: A Go feature flag never adopts, restarts, reports as Go-owned or terminates an old Node process.
runtime_ownership.go_processes_after_go_disable: A disabled feature flag never makes Node adopt, restart, report as Node-owned or terminate a Go process.

feature_switch.script: go_scripts_api controls Script run, stop, run log and Operation/SSE paths as one capability.
feature_switch.executor: go_executors_api controls Executor run, action, stop, run log and Operation/SSE paths as one capability.
feature_switch.legacy_crud: No P12 switch changes create, update, delete, toggle or import persistence semantics.
feature_switch.fallback: A disabled capability may choose the legacy Node implementation only before accepting work. It may not retry an accepted Go operation through Node or vice versa.

accepted_identifiers.project_scope_rule: The project must exist and the persisted Script or Executor definition must be loaded by project_id plus its persisted resource id. MCP label lookup is a server-side lookup constrained to the same project and resolves to that persisted executor id before execution.

authorization_boundary.rule: Client-supplied run/stop/action fields cannot prove project ownership, process ownership, execution authorization or a resource definition. The service ignores or rejects them and never uses a client pid to kill a process.

persistent_definition.source_rule: inline writes a runtime-owned temporary script file; file resolves the persisted source path. Go must preserve source selection and interpretation semantics while applying its controlled file and process policy.
persistent_definition.work_dir_rule: ${workspace} and ${planDir} placeholders resolve from trusted project context. A client run request does not override persisted work_dir.

context_inject.rule: Context originates from trusted loop execution context. Manual input cannot claim a hook, schedule stage or another resource's context.

trigger_behavior.manual: loads one project-owned persisted script and returns the completed legacy ScriptRunResult after its Node operation settles.
trigger_behavior.hook: runs enabled hook scripts matching stage in sort_order then id order. A single failure is recorded; only validation:before with fail_aborts=1 aborts the current hook stage.
trigger_behavior.schedule: accepts only enabled schedule scripts with valid five-field cron. A script runs at most once in the same minute according to last_run_at; invalid cron is skipped and reported by the scheduler path.
trigger_behavior.hook_failure: hook query and execution errors do not leak into a client supplied command or change process ownership.

run_stop_log.node_stop_return: scripts:stop returns AppSnapshot after requesting cancellation; the legacy stop request does not accept pid, command, env, cwd, trigger or manual ownership proof.
run_stop_log.status_mapping: exit code 0 is ok; all other returned exit codes are bad. Node cancellation is represented by the runner completion path rather than a separate Script stopped state.
run_stop_log.log: last_log stores the trailing at most 24000 characters of combined output and error text. Event metadata carries a log file reference when present, not the log tail itself.
run_stop_log.migration_rule: Go exposes bounded redacted stream metadata and a safe tail summary; raw output, command, env, path and inline source never enter Operation, SSE, snapshots, errors or fixture data.

persistent_definition.normalization: camelCase and selected snake_case persisted aliases normalize to one definition. Duplicate labels in one project are rejected on create/update; tasks.json import may deterministically deduplicate labels.
persistent_definition.tasks_json: Only .vscode/tasks.json tasks[] is imported; launch.json debug configuration is ignored and debug-only fields are rejected or skipped.

dependency_behavior.lookup: Dependencies resolve by persisted label inside the same project.
dependency_behavior.parallel: Dependencies begin together and their result order follows the configured dependency label order.
dependency_behavior.sequence: Dependencies run in configured order and stop at the first non-ok result.
dependency_behavior.sharing: One root execution context memoizes an in-progress and completed dependency by executor id, so a shared dependency runs once for that root execution.
dependency_behavior.aggregation: A failed dependency produces a root bad or stopped result with compact dependency results; it does not execute the dependent command.

plugin_lifecycle.start: requires a persisted plugin start command and permits at most one live process for project_id plus executor_id in the owning runtime.
plugin_lifecycle.reload: uses persisted input or command action only while the owning runtime has a live registered plugin process.
plugin_lifecycle.stop: uses the persisted stop action when configured, then owner-controlled graceful and forced termination. It never accepts a client pid or plugin state as authority.

run_stop_log.node_stop_return: executors:stop returns AppSnapshot after requesting a project-owned existing operation or plugin stop. The MCP legacy summary includes only stopped count and a safe snapshot summary.
run_stop_log.log: lastLog and plugin log buffer retain the trailing at most 24000 characters. The migration records truncation metadata rather than silently treating a shortened tail as full output.

snapshot_live_state.scripts: Snapshot keeps project-owned script rows in sort_order then id order and preserves the legacy snake_case Script fields including last_*.
snapshot_live_state.executors: executorFromRow creates camelCase Executor fields. executorSnapshotRow additionally exposes selected snake_case aliases, running, runStatus and activeOperation.
snapshot_live_state.live_state_rule: running and activeOperation reflect only an operation registered by the active runtime for the same project and executor. Persisted plugin_state is not evidence that a process from another runtime is owned or live.

operation_and_sse.wire_naming: HTTP, MCP and SSE operation fields use snake_case. Renderer adapters may add camelCase aliases without changing values, ownership or authorization.
operation_and_sse.legacy_to_operation: Legacy Node IPC returns a completed ScriptRunResult, ExecutorRunResult or AppSnapshot. Once the matching Go capability is enabled, run, stop and executor action return OperationAccepted and snapshots/SSE converge to the same authoritative Operation.
operation_and_sse.event_rule: Each event for an operation carries the same operation_id, project_id and runtime_owner. Reconnect reads the same operation and does not repeat a run, action or stop request.

src/loop/snapshots.js: 'SELECT * FROM scripts WHERE project_id = ? ORDER BY sort_order ASC, id ASC'

inventory.drift_rule: A missing source marker, changed runtime scope, absent ownership flag, unclassified controlled action or weakened identifier-only authorization boundary fails the P12 inventory until this reviewed contract is intentionally updated.
